mechanism_narrative: Monthly industrial production growth positively predicts S&P 500 forward returns because rising production signals expanding corporate revenues and earnings capacity.
failure_condition: Positive 20d forward returns occur in less than 55% of months when INDPRO is rising, or rank correlation between INDPRO change and 20d return is negative over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, the restrictive policy level elevates corporate borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 20d returns in more than 50% of periods when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in nonfarm payrolls and an increase in the federal funds rate signals a restrictive monetary policy hitting labor markets, which amplifies recession risk and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling PAYEMS and rising FEDFUNDS fails to produce negative 60d returns in more than 60% of occurrences, or the interaction term rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During high interest rate regimes, declines in real retail sales signal weakening consumer demand that directly reduces corporate revenue forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy environment with FEDFUNDS above 4 percent
failure_condition: Negative 20d returns follow declining RSAFS in less than 55% of high-rate regime months, or the strategy Sharpe ratio is below 0.2 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.3 percentage point increase in the unemployment rate over a three-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce household income growth and compress corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 60d S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time, or the mean forward return is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts below 1.5 million units indicates contracting residential investment that drags construction and materials sectors, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5d returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered months, or the rank IC between HOUST level and 5d return is positive over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling retail sales indicates a profit margin squeeze from cost-push pressures without matching demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint condition of rising INDPRO and falling RSAFS produces positive 20d returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction rank IC is above zero over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained elevation in the CPI inflation index above its 12-month trend signals persistent inflationary pressure that erodes real earnings and prompts further Federal Reserve tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL exceeds its 12-month trailing average by more than 0.5 index points
failure_condition: Triggered high-inflation episodes produce positive 60d S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time, or the rank correlation between CPI deviation and forward return is positive over a 3-year window.
